extra.reasoning: The property requires that in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false. Transition [t5] "on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 12): sent := true" directly sets sent true while opted_out is true, so the only question is reachability of its guard. The guard requires opted_out=true AND ticks>=12. Both are reachable: one optout event sets opted_out=true (and sent=false), and twelve tick events drive ticks from 0 up to 12 (capped at 12), each tick also resetting sent=false. After that prefix the state is opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=12. A send_attempt then enables t5 (its guard opted_out and ticks>=12 holds), which sets sent:=true. Although t3 (guard opted_out) is also enabled on that send_attempt, t5 is an enabled matching transition, so an execution exists in which t5 fires, producing the state opted_out=true AND sent=true — violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). The witnessing sequence is 14 events (1 optout + 12 tick + 1 send_attempt), within the 16-event bound. Therefore the safety property is violated.
